Travel Search AppBlog

🏨 10 Tips to Find the Cheapest Hotels Anywhere

2026-03-06
hotels
budget
tips

Hotels are often the biggest travel expense — bigger than flights. But with the right approach, you can cut your hotel costs by 30-50%. Here are 10 proven strategies.

1. Compare Multiple Platforms

Never book on the first site you check. Prices for the same room can vary 20-40% between platforms. Always compare at least Booking.com and one aggregator like Hotellook.

Compare hotel prices

2. Book 3-6 Weeks in Advance

Too early = high prices (hotels haven't started competing yet). Too late = limited options. The sweet spot is 3-6 weeks before your trip.

3. Stay on Weekdays

Business hotels are cheaper on weekends, tourist hotels are cheaper on weekdays. Know which type your hotel is and plan accordingly.

4. Look Beyond the City Center

Hotels 15-20 minutes from the center can be 50% cheaper. If public transport is good (Tokyo, London, Paris), this is a no-brainer.

5. Use Hotel Loyalty Programs

Even free tiers give you 10-15% discounts and perks like free breakfast and late checkout. Sign up before booking.

6. Check for Free Cancellation

Book a refundable rate first, then keep looking for better deals. If you find one, cancel the first booking.

7. Consider Apartments for 3+ Nights

For longer stays, apartments on Booking.com are often 30-40% cheaper per night than hotels, plus you save on food with a kitchen.

8. Travel in Shoulder Season

April-May and September-October offer the best combo: good weather + low hotel prices. Avoid school holidays.

9. Contact Hotels Directly

Some hotels offer better rates if you book directly (they save on platform commissions). Always worth checking their website.

10. Use Price Alerts

Set up alerts on comparison sites. Hotel prices fluctuate daily — catching a price drop can save you 20% or more.

Start comparing prices now and find the best deals for your next trip!
